name: Mung bean (food medium)
basis: per 100 g raw mature dry seed (Vigna radiata)

components.name: starch
components.source: USDA FDC 174256 (Mung beans, mature seeds, raw; total carbohydrate 62.6 g/100g minus fiber 16.3 and free sugars 6.6)
components.evidence: Dominant carbon/energy reservoir of the dry seed; α-glucan hydrolysing to glucose is the principal fermentable carbon source once amylolysed.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Tajoddin & Shinde, The Bioscan 2014 (mung cultivars, sucrose 1.06-2.19 g/100g); Kuo et al. 1988 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf00079a008
components.evidence: Principal free disaccharide (glucose+fructose) of the raw seed; midpoint of reported cultivar range.

components.name: stachyose
components.source: Tajoddin & Shinde, The Bioscan 2014 (stachyose 0.50-1.50 g/100g); Kuo et al. 1988 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf00079a008
components.evidence: Dominant raffinose-family oligosaccharide (α-galactosyl); fermentable by α-galactosidase-positive microbes, midpoint of range.

components.name: raffinose
components.source: Tajoddin & Shinde, The Bioscan 2014 (raffinose 0.38-0.69 g/100g); Kuo et al. 1988 JAFC doi:10.1021/jf00079a008
components.evidence: Raffinose-family oligosaccharide, midpoint of cultivar range.

components.name: verbascose
components.source: Kuo, VanMiddlesworth & Wolf 1988, J Agric Food Chem 36:32 doi:10.1021/jf00079a008 (verbascose a prominent RFO of mung/green gram)
components.evidence: Higher raffinose-family oligosaccharide (pentasaccharide) reported as a prominent RFO in mung bean; value is a literature-typical estimate (poorly resolved in seed panels).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free glucose is minor in the intact raw seed (sugars dominated by sucrose+RFOs) but is the monomer released on starch/sucrose/RFO hydrolysis; present, not individually quantified.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Minor free monosaccharide in the raw seed; released with glucose on sucrose hydrolysis.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Not free in the seed; released by α-galactosidase from the raffinose-family oligosaccharides. Present via hydrolysis, not quantified.

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: Devi et al. 2025, mung bean varieties profiling, PMC11854456 (phytic acid 3745-5175 µg/g = 374-518 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Quantitatively the dominant organic acid of the seed and its principal phosphorus store; a chelator of Ca/Fe/Zn (antinutrient) and a phosphate source once phytase-liberated. Midpoint of reported range.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Ganesan & Xu 2018, Food Sci Human Wellness doi:10.1016/j.fshw.2017.11.002 (malic + quinic = 55-86% of total organic acids); Guajardo-Flores et al. 2013 doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2012.10.001
components.evidence: One of the two dominant free organic acids of mung bean/sprout; MISSING from FooDB profile. Present, seed fresh-weight amount not cleanly quantified in the literature.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Ganesan & Xu 2018, Food Sci Human Wellness doi:10.1016/j.fshw.2017.11.002 (co-dominant with malic, 55-86% of total organic acids)
components.evidence: Co-dominant free organic acid; MISSING from FooDB profile. Present, not individually quantified in the raw seed.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Guajardo-Flores et al. 2013 Food Chem doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2012.10.001 (HPLC-UV organic-acid panel of V. radiata)
components.evidence: Present in the mung bean organic-acid profile; MISSING from FooDB. Not individually quantified for the seed.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Guajardo-Flores et al. 2013 Food Chem doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2012.10.001
components.evidence: Present in the mung bean HPLC organic-acid panel; MISSING from FooDB. Trace, unquantified.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Guajardo-Flores et al. 2013 Food Chem doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2012.10.001
components.evidence: Detected in mung bean organic-acid profile; trace, unquantified.

components.name: aconitic acid
components.source: Guajardo-Flores et al. 2013 Food Chem doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2012.10.001
components.evidence: Detected in the 7-8 acid mung bean HPLC panel; trace.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Devi et al. 2025 profiling, PMC11854456 (oxalate 0.35-0.72 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Low soluble-oxalate content; minor organic acid, midpoint of reported range.

components.name: L-glutamate (glutamic acid)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid of the seed protein; major N and skeletal-carbon source (protein-hydrolysate value).

components.name: L-aspartate (aspartic acid)
components.evidence: Second most abundant amino acid.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Legume seed is lysine-rich (complements cereal diets).

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Sulfur amino acid; limiting AA of legume protein.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Second sulfur amino acid (as cystine dimer); co-limiting with methionine.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: High-protein legume seed; amino acids above are the protein-hydrolysate profile and the dominant N source.

components.name: thiamin (B1)
components.evidence: Legume seed is thiamin-rich.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Very high food folate (DFE); a defining micronutrient of the dry seed.

components.name: vitamin A (RAE, from provitamin-A carotenoids)
components.evidence: As carotenoids (beta-carotene ~68 µg, alpha-carotene ~5 µg); no preformed retinol in this plant food.

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: Absent (plant food); required as supplement for cobalamin auxotrophs.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation of the dry seed.

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.evidence: Large fraction bound as phytate (see phytic acid); phytase-liberated Pi is bioavailable.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: High non-heme iron (partly phytate-chelated).

components.name: sulfur
components.evidence: Organic S carried mainly in the sulfur amino acids methionine/cystine plus inorganic sulfate; growth-relevant S source, not separately quantified in FDC.

components.name: molybdenum (molybdate)
components.source: FooDB FOOD00200; legume seed micronutrient literature
components.evidence: Genuine trace micronutrient (nitrogenase/nitrate-reductase cofactor, elevated in legumes); present but unquantified in FDC.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Principal polyunsaturated fatty acid of the low-fat seed (total fat 1.15 g/100g).

components.name: saturated fatty acids (chiefly palmitic 16:0)
components.evidence: Total SFA; dominated by palmitate.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Dry seed; low moisture.

components.name: dietary fiber (cell-wall polysaccharide)
components.evidence: Non-starch structural polysaccharide; partly fermentable by fiber-degrading microbes.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid soil contaminant, not a growth nutrient; FooDB trace

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal) vitamin A; absent in this plant seed, which supplies only provitamin-A carotenoids; FooDB predicted

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: bacterial-fermentation food-preservative polymer, not a native mung bean constituent; FooDB implausible/predicted

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: non-biological D/allo stereochemistry artifact duplicating L-isoleucine; not a real seed metabolite

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/citrus, biologically implausible in a legume seed; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Ascorbigen
remove.reason: indole-ascorbate adduct derived from Brassica glucobrassicin; implausible in a legume (non-Brassica); FooDB predicted

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: trace volatile flavour aldehyde, not a growth nutrient

missing_added: phytic acid (~446 mg/100g) - dominant organic acid and phosphorus store, absent from FooDB profile
malic + quinic acid (co-dominant organic acids, 55-86% of total), citric, succinic, fumaric, aconitic, oxalic - organic acids missing from FooDB
quantitative free-sugar / RFO split: sucrose ~1.5 > stachyose ~1.0 > verbascose ~1.5 > raffinose 0.53 g/100g
starch (~46 g/100g) - the dominant fermentable carbon reservoir
full amino-acid profile with amounts and total protein (23.86 g/100g)
full vitamin panel with amounts (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B9/C/E/K, choline; B12 confirmed absent)
individual fatty acids and total fat
keep_uncovered: Genistein, Isoflavones (daidzein-type) - genuine legume isoflavones (no BiGG met)
Vitexin / isovitexin - signature mung bean C-glycosyl flavones (present in seed coat)
p-Coumaric acid, ferulic/caffeic phenolic acids - genuine phenolics
alpha-carotene, beta-carotene, beta-cryptoxanthin - genuine carotenoids (provitamin A carried above as RAE)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 174256 (Mung beans, mature seeds, raw; SR Legacy) - full nutrient panel: sugars, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins
Devi et al. 2025, Profiling the Nutritional, Phytochemical and Functional Properties of Mung Bean Varieties, PMC11854456 (phytic acid, oxalate, proximates, amino acids)
Tajoddin & Shinde 2014, The Bioscan (raffinose/stachyose/sucrose contents of mung bean cultivars)
Kuo, VanMiddlesworth & Wolf 1988, J Agric Food Chem 36:32 doi:10.1021/jf00079a008 (raffinose-family oligosaccharides incl. verbascose in seeds)
Ganesan & Xu 2018, Food Sci Human Wellness doi:10.1016/j.fshw.2017.11.002 (mung bean phytochemical review; malic+quinic dominant organic acids)
Guajardo-Flores et al. 2013, Food Chem doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2012.10.001 (HPLC-UV organic-acid panel of V. radiata sprouts)
notes: Dry high-protein/high-starch legume seed: the dominant carbon source is starch (~46 g/100g) hydrolysing to glucose, backed by sucrose plus the raffinose-family oligosaccharides (stachyose/verbascose/raffinose) that need α-galactosidase; the dominant nitrogen source is the 23.9 g/100g protein (glutamate- and aspartate-rich, lysine-rich, Met/Cys-limited). Organic acids - entirely absent from the FooDB profile - were added: phytic acid (~446 mg/100g) is quantitatively dominant and also the main P store, with malic and quinic the leading free acids. Removed 4 toxic heavy metals/metalloids, preformed retinol, and 4 implausible/predicted FooDB compounds (epsilon-polylysine, erythro-isoleucine, oxypeucedanin, ascorbigen).
